# File_Last_Modified_Date

#--------------------
# Title
#   Study_Name: Soreq Cave, Israel Speleothem Oxygen Isotope Data 33.8-4.4 ky BP
#--------------------
# Investigators
#   Investigators: Orland, I.J.; Bar-Matthews, M.; Ayalon, A.; Matthews, A.; Kozdon, R.; Ushikubo, T.; Valley, J.W.
#--------------------
# Description_Notes_and_Keywords
#   Description: Oxygen isotope data and age model from sample 2N of Soreq Cave near Jerusalem, Israel. Provided Keywords: oxygen isotopes, seasonality, SIMS
#--------------------
# Publication 
#   Authors: Ian J. Orland, Miryam Bar-Matthews, Avner Ayalon, Alan Matthews, Reinhard Kozdon, Takayuki Ushikubo, John W. Valley
#   Published_Date_or_Year: 2012-07-15
#   Published_Title: Seasonal resolution of Eastern Mediterranean climate change since 34 ka from a Soreq Cave speleothem
#   Journal_Name: Geochimica et Cosmochimica Acta
#   Volume: 89
#   Edition: 
#   Issue: 
#   Pages: 240-255
#   Report_Number: 
#   DOI: 10.1016/j.gca.2012.04.035
#   Online_Resource: http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0016703712002402
#   Full_Citation:
#   Abstract: The combination of ion microprobe analysis of d18O and confocal laser fluorescent microscope imaging of annual growth bands in a Soreq Cave speleothem provides sub-annual-scale climate information between 34 and 4 ka. This high-resolution methodology is ideal both for comparing seasonal climate patterns across broad windows of time and examining rapid climate events, such as the Younger Dryas termination, in detail. The sub-annual d18O gradients we report represent a combination of seasonal variability in rainfall amount and air temperature. A distinct change in both the pattern of fluorescent banding and the gradient of d18O measured in situ across single, annual growth bands indicates a change in seasonal climate patterns of the Eastern Mediterranean region following Heinrich event 1 and again after the Younger Dryas. Throughout the Holocene, wet winters and dry summers characterized regional climate. During the Younger Dryas, we find that regional climate may have been more arid than in the Holocene, but the fluorescent banding pattern indicates that the supply of dripwater to the cave was more consistent year-round. We suggest that a reduced gradient of seasonal precipitation, occasional snowfall, and vegetation differences may have all contributed to the isotope and fluorescent banding patterns observed during Heinrich event 1 and the last glacial stadial. Detailed investigation of the Younger Dryas termination reveals a rapid onset of regional environmental change. Fluorescent band counting indicates that the Younger Dryas termination, as recorded by rainfall in the Eastern Mediterranean, spanned a minimum of 12 years.
#--------------------
# Funding_Agency
#  Funding_Agency_Name: US National Science Foundation
#  Grant: AGS-10-03487, EAR-08-38058, EAR-03-19230, EAR-07-44079, EAR-10-53466

#   Elevation_m: 400

#   Last_Year: 4440
#   Time_Unit: cal yr BP
#   Core_Length_m: 
#   Parameter_Keywords: 
#   Notes:
#--------------------
# Chronology_Information
#   Chronology: Uranium-Thorium
#     Chronology_Download_Resource: https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/pub/data/paleo/templates/noaa-wds-paleo-uth-terms.csv
#   Chronology_Download_Description: Uranium-Thorium terms and definitions.
#   Chronology_Notes: New U–Th disequilibrium ages of sample 2N acquired by multi-collector inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometer analysis at the Geological Survey of Israel. 
#   Rejection_Rationale:
#   238U_Decay_Constant:
#   234U_Decay_Constant:
#   230Th_Decay_Constant:
#   Initial_230Th/232Th: 1.69
#   Initial_230Th/232Th_Method: established in the detailed isochron study of Soreq Cave speleothems by Kaufman et al. (1998)
#   Age_Model_Method: linear interpolation between 11 selected U–Th dates
#   Missing_Values: na
#   Chronology_Table:
# samp_id	depth_top_mm	U_ppm	U_2s_ppm	234U_238U_act	234U_238U_act_2s	230Th_234U_act	230Th_234U_act_2s	230Th_232Th_act	230Th_232Th_act_2s	age_uncorr_BM	age_uncorr_2s_yr	age_corr_BP1950	age_corr_2s_yr
# 2N-1	4.6	0.4200	0.0003	1.02401	0.00120	0.04735	0.00070	13.10	0.20	5300	300	4500	200
# 2N-3B	13.7	0.3898	0.0003	1.01753	0.00113	0.05845	0.00094	10.29	0.17	6500	200	5400	300
# 2N-12	40.8	0.3536	0.0004	1.04713	0.00150	0.08501	0.00121	5.05	0.08	9700	280	6300	400

# Data variables follow that are preceded by '##' in columns one and two.
# Variables list, one per line, shortname-tab-var components: what, material, error, units, seasonality, data type, detail, method, C or N for Character or Numeric data)
#
## depth_mm	depth,,,millimeter,,speleothems,,,N,horizontal distance in mm from the edge of the sample 2N slab
## age_yrBP	age,,,calendar year before present,,speleothems,,,N, present defined as 1950 CE; age model is a linear interpolation between U-Th ages; linear extrapolation of observed growth rates is extended to data not bracketed by dates
## d18O_VPDB	delta 18O,calcite,,per mil VPDB,,speleothems,,multiple collector secondary ion mass spectrometry,N,10um-diameter analysis spots
## d18O_VPDB_err	delta 18O,calcite,two standard deviations,per mil VPDB,,speleothems,,multiple collector secondary ion mass spectrometry,N,spot-to-spot reproducibility; 10um-diameter analysis spots
